questions.question: How many triangles appear after the third step of the Sierpinski Triangle construction?
How many squares remain after the second step in the Sierpinski Carpet?
What happens to the perimeter of the Koch Snowflake as iterations continue?
What happens to the area of the Koch Snowflake despite increasing perimeter?
questions.answer: The area approaches a finite value.

questions.question: Which mathematical idea explains the infinite complexity of fractals?
What is the number of edges in a pentagonal prism?
How many faces does a pentagonal pyramid have?
What is the relationship between vertices, edges and faces in polyhedra?
Why is unfolding a cube into a net useful for shortest path problems?
questions.answer: It converts surface paths into straight lines.

questions.question: What geometric concept explains that different solids may share the same projection?
questions.answer: Loss of depth information in projection.

questions.question: Why are three views often required in orthographic projection?
questions.answer: To completely represent the length, width and height of an object.

questions.question: What property ensures that lengths remain proportional in isometric drawings?
What angle separates the three axes in isometric projection?
Why do circles appear as ellipses in isometric drawings?
questions.answer: Because of the angled projection of circular faces.

questions.question: What mathematical pattern describes the number of triangles in Sierpinski construction?
What mathematical pattern describes the number of squares in the Sierpinski Carpet?
How does recursion help construct fractals?
questions.answer: It repeatedly applies the same geometric rule.

questions.question: What real-life structures resemble fractal patterns?
Why are fractals useful in computer graphics?
questions.answer: They help model complex natural shapes realistically.
